NKJV.1: And I, brethren, could not speak to you as to spiritual people but as to carnal, as to babes in Christ.
NKJV.2: I fed you with milk and not with solid food; for until now you were not able to receive it, and even now you are still not able;
NKJV.3: for you are still carnal. For where there are envy, strife, and divisions among you, are you not carnal and behaving like mere men?
NKJV.4: For when one says, “I am of Paul,” and another, “I am of Apollos,” are you not carnal?
NKJV.5: Who then is Paul, and who is Apollos, but ministers through whom you believed, as the Lord gave to each one?
NKJV.6: I planted, Apollos watered, but God gave the increase.
NKJV.7: So then neither he who plants is anything, nor he who waters, but God who gives the increase.
NKJV.8: Now he who plants and he who waters are one, and each one will receive his own reward according to his own labor.
NKJV.9: For we are God’s fellow workers; you are God’s field, you are God’s building.
NKJV.10: According to the grace of God which was given to me, as a wise master builder I have laid the foundation, and another builds on it. But let each one take heed how he builds on it.
NKJV.11: For no other foundation can anyone lay than that which is laid, which is Jesus Christ.
NKJV.12: Now if anyone builds on this foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, straw,
NKJV.13: each one’s work will become clear; for the Day will declare it, because it will be revealed by fire; and the fire will test each one’s work, of what sort it is.
NKJV.14: If anyone’s work which he has built on it endures, he will receive a reward.
NKJV.15: If anyone’s work is burned, he will suffer loss; but he himself will be saved, yet so as through fire.
NKJV.16: Do you not know that you are the temple of God and that the Spirit of God dwells in you?
NKJV.17: If anyone defiles the temple of God, God will destroy him. For the temple of God is holy, which temple you are.
NKJV.18: Let no one deceive himself. If anyone among you seems to be wise in this age, let him become a fool that he may become wise.
NKJV.19: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, “He catches the wise in their own craftiness”;
NKJV.20: and again, “The LORD knows the thoughts of the wise, that they are futile.”
NKJV.21: Therefore let no one boast in men. For all things are yours:
NKJV.22: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas, or the world or life or death, or things present or things to come—all are yours.
NKJV.23: And you are Christ’s, and Christ is God’s.

ESV.1: But I, brothers,  could not address you as spiritual people, but as people of the flesh, as infants in Christ.
ESV.2: I fed you with milk, not solid food, for you were not ready for it. And even now you are not yet ready,
ESV.3: for you are still of the flesh. For while there is jealousy and strife among you, are you not of the flesh and behaving only in a human way?
ESV.4: For when one says, “I follow Paul,” and another, “I follow Apollos,” are you not being merely human?
ESV.5: What then is Apollos? What is Paul? Servants through whom you believed, as the Lord assigned to each.
ESV.6: I planted, Apollos watered, but God gave the growth.
ESV.7: So neither he who plants nor he who waters is anything, but only God who gives the growth.
ESV.8: He who plants and he who waters are one, and each will receive his wages according to his labor.
ESV.9: For we are God's fellow workers. You are God's field, God's building.
ESV.10: According to the grace of God given to me, like a skilled  master builder I laid a foundation, and someone else is building upon it. Let each one take care how he builds upon it.
ESV.11: For no one can lay a foundation other than that which is laid, which is Jesus Christ.
ESV.12: Now if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, straw —
ESV.13: each one's work will become manifest, for the Day will disclose it, because it will be revealed by fire, and the fire will test what sort of work each one has done.
ESV.14: If the work that anyone has built on the foundation survives, he will receive a reward.
ESV.15: If anyone's work is burned up, he will suffer loss, though he himself will be saved, but only as through fire.
ESV.16: Do you not know that you  are God's temple and that God's Spirit dwells in you?
ESV.17: If anyone destroys God's temple, God will destroy him. For God's temple is holy, and you are that temple.
ESV.18: Let no one deceive himself. If anyone among you thinks that he is wise in this age, let him become a fool that he may become wise.
ESV.19: For the wisdom of this world is folly with God. For it is written, “He catches the wise in their craftiness,”
ESV.20: and again, “The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise, that they are futile.”
ESV.21: So let no one boast in men. For all things are yours,
ESV.22: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas or the world or life or death or the present or the future — all are yours,
ESV.23: and you are Christ's, and Christ is God's.

NASB.1: And I, brethren, could not speak to you as to spiritual men, but as to men of flesh, as to infants in Christ.
NASB.2: I gave you milk to drink, not solid food; for you were not yet able [to receive it]. Indeed, even now you are not yet able,
NASB.3: for you are still fleshly. For since there is jealousy and strife among you, are you not fleshly, and are you not walking like mere men?
NASB.4: For when one says, 'I am of Paul,' and another, 'I am of Apollos,' are you not [mere] men?
NASB.5: What then is Apollos? And what is Paul? Servants through whom you believed, even as the Lord gave [opportunity] to each one.
NASB.6: I planted, Apollos watered, but God was causing the growth.
NASB.7: So then neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but God who causes the growth.
NASB.8: Now he who plants and he who waters are one; but each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.
NASB.9: For we are God's fellow workers; you are God's field, God's building.
NASB.10: According to the grace of God which was given to me, like a wise master builder I laid a foundation, and another is building on it. But each man must be careful how he builds on it.
NASB.11: For no man can lay a foundation other than the one which is laid, which is Jesus Christ.
NASB.12: Now if any man builds on the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, straw,
NASB.13: each man's work will become evident; for the day will show it because it is [to be] revealed with fire, and the fire itself will test the quality of each man's work.
NASB.14: If any man's work which he has built on it remains, he will receive a reward.
NASB.15: If any man's work is burned up, he will suffer loss; but he himself will be saved, yet so as through fire.
NASB.16: Do you not know that you are a temple of God and [that] the Spirit of God dwells in you?
NASB.17: If any man destroys the temple of God, God will destroy him, for the temple of God is holy, and that is what you are.
NASB.18: Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you thinks that he is wise in this age, he must become foolish, so that he may become wise.
NASB.19: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness before God. For it is written, '[He is] THE ONE WHO CATCHES THE WISE IN THEIR CRAFTINESS';
NASB.20: and again, 'THE LORD KNOWS THE REASONINGS of the wise, THAT THEY ARE USELESS.'
NASB.21: So then let no one boast in men. For all things belong to you,
NASB.22: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas or the world or life or death or things present or things to come; all things belong to you,
NASB.23: and you belong to Christ; and Christ belongs to God.

NLT.1: Dear brothers and sisters, when I was with you I couldn’t talk to you as I would to spiritual people. I had to talk as though you belonged to this world or as though you were infants in Christ.
NLT.2: I had to feed you with milk, not with solid food, because you weren’t ready for anything stronger. And you still aren’t ready,
NLT.3: for you are still controlled by your sinful nature. You are jealous of one another and quarrel with each other. Doesn’t that prove you are controlled by your sinful nature? Aren’t you living like people of the world?
NLT.4: When one of you says, “I am a follower of Paul,” and another says, “I follow Apollos,” aren’t you acting just like people of the world?
NLT.5: After all, who is Apollos? Who is Paul? We are only God’s servants through whom you believed the Good News. Each of us did the work the Lord gave us.
NLT.6: I planted the seed in your hearts, and Apollos watered it, but it was God who made it grow.
NLT.7: It’s not important who does the planting, or who does the watering. What’s important is that God makes the seed grow.
NLT.8: The one who plants and the one who waters work together with the same purpose. And both will be rewarded for their own hard work.
NLT.9: For we are both God’s workers. And you are God’s field. You are God’s building.
NLT.10: Because of God’s grace to me, I have laid the foundation like an expert builder. Now others are building on it. But whoever is building on this foundation must be very careful.
NLT.11: For no one can lay any foundation other than the one we already have — Jesus Christ.
NLT.12: Anyone who builds on that foundation may use a variety of materials — gold, silver, jewels, wood, hay, or straw.
NLT.13: But on the judgment day, fire will reveal what kind of work each builder has done. The fire will show if a person’s work has any value.
NLT.14: If the work survives, that builder will receive a reward.
NLT.15: But if the work is burned up, the builder will suffer great loss. The builder will be saved, but like someone barely escaping through a wall of flames.
NLT.16: Don’t you realize that all of you together are the temple of God and that the Spirit of God lives in you?
NLT.17: God will destroy anyone who destroys this temple. For God’s temple is holy, and you are that temple.
NLT.18: Stop deceiving yourselves. If you think you are wise by this world’s standards, you need to become a fool to be truly wise.
NLT.19:
For the wisdom of this world is foolishness to God. As the Scriptures say, “He traps the wise
in the snare of their own cleverness.”
NLT.20:
And again, “The LORD knows the thoughts of the wise;
he knows they are worthless.”
NLT.21: So don’t boast about following a particular human leader. For everything belongs to you —
NLT.22: whether Paul or Apollos or Peter, or the world, or life and death, or the present and the future. Everything belongs to you,
NLT.23: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.

WEB.1: Brothers, I couldn’t speak to you as to spiritual, but as to fleshly, as to babies in Christ.
WEB.2: I fed you with milk, not with meat; for you weren’t yet ready. Indeed, not even now are you ready,
WEB.3: for you are still fleshly. For insofar as there is jealousy, strife, and factions among you, aren’t you fleshly, and don’t you walk in the ways of men?
WEB.4: For when one says, “I follow Paul,” and another, “I follow Apollos,” aren’t you fleshly?
WEB.5: Who then is Apollos, and who is Paul, but servants through whom you believed; and each as the Lord gave to him?
WEB.6: I planted. Apollos watered. But God gave the increase.
WEB.7: So then neither he who plants is anything, nor he who waters, but God who gives the increase.
WEB.8: Now he who plants and he who waters are the same, but each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.
WEB.9: For we are God’s fellow workers. You are God’s farming, God’s building.
WEB.10: According to the grace of God which was given to me, as a wise master builder I laid a foundation, and another builds on it. But let each man be careful how he builds on it.
WEB.11: For no one can lay any other foundation than that which has been laid, which is Jesus Christ.
WEB.12: But if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, costly stones, wood, hay, or stubble;
WEB.13: each man’s work will be revealed. For the Day will declare it, because it is revealed in fire; and the fire itself will test what sort of work each man’s work is.
WEB.14: If any man’s work remains which he built on it, he will receive a reward.
WEB.15: If any man’s work is burned, he will suffer loss, but he himself will be saved, but as through fire.
WEB.16: Don’t you know that you are a temple of God, and that God’s Spirit lives in you?
WEB.17: If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy him; for God’s temple is holy, which you are.
WEB.18: Let no one deceive himself. If anyone thinks that he is wise among you in this world, let him become a fool, that he may become wise.
WEB.19: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, “He has taken the wise in their craftiness.”
WEB.20: And again, “The Lord knows the reasoning of the wise, that it is worthless.”
WEB.21: Therefore let no one boast in men. For all things are yours,
WEB.22: whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come. All are yours,
WEB.23: and you are Christ’s, and Christ is God’s.

CSB17.1: For my part, brothers and sisters, I was not able to speak to you as spiritual people but as people of the flesh, as babies in Christ. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.2: I gave you milk to drink, not solid food, since you were not yet ready for it. In fact, you are still not ready,
CSB17.3: because you are still worldly. For since there is envy <sup>ⓘ</sup> and strife <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> among you, are you not worldly and behaving like mere humans?
CSB17.4: For whenever someone says, “I belong to Paul,” and another, “I belong to Apollos,” <sup>ⓚ</sup> are you not acting like mere humans?
CSB17.5: What then is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants through whom you believed, and each has the role the Lord has given.
CSB17.6: I planted, <sup>ⓛ</sup> Apollos watered, but God gave the growth.
CSB17.7: So then neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but only God who gives the growth.
CSB17.8: Now he who plants and he who waters are one, <sup>[2]</sup> and each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.
CSB17.9: For we are God’s coworkers. <sup>[3]</sup> You are God’s field, God’s building. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.10: According to God’s grace that was given to me, I have laid a foundation as a skilled master builder, <sup>[4]</sup> and another builds on it. But each one is to be careful how he builds on it.
CSB17.11: For no one can lay any other foundation <sup>ⓝ</sup> than what has been laid down. That foundation is Jesus Christ.
CSB17.12: If anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, <sup>ⓐ</sup> costly stones, <sup>ⓑ</sup> wood, hay, or straw,
CSB17.13: each one’s work will become obvious. For the day will disclose it, because it will be revealed by fire; <sup>ⓒ</sup> the fire will test the quality of each one’s work. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.14: If anyone’s work that he has built survives, he will receive a reward.
CSB17.15: If anyone’s work is burned up, he will experience <sup>[5]</sup> loss, but he himself will be saved <sup>ⓔ</sup>  — but only as through fire. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.16: Don’t you yourselves know that you are God’s temple <sup>ⓖ</sup> and that the Spirit of God lives in you? <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.17: If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy him; <sup>ⓘ</sup> for God’s temple is holy, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and that is what you are.
CSB17.18: Let no one deceive <sup>ⓚ</sup> himself. If anyone among you thinks he is wise in this age, <sup>ⓛ</sup> let him become a fool <sup>ⓜ</sup> so that he can become wise. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.19: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness ⓐ with God, since it is written, ⓑ He catches the wise in their craftiness; [6] ⓒ
CSB17.20: and again, The Lord knows that the reasonings ⓓ of the wise are futile. [7] ⓔ
CSB17.21: So let no one boast in human leaders, for everything is yours <sup>ⓕ</sup>  —
CSB17.22: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas <sup>ⓖ</sup> or the world or life <sup>ⓗ</sup> or death <sup>ⓘ</sup> or things present or things to come <sup>ⓙ</sup>  — everything is yours,
CSB17.23: and you belong to Christ, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and Christ belongs to God.

AMP.1: However, brothers and sisters, I could not talk to you as to spiritual people, but [only] as to worldly people [dominated by human nature], mere infants [in the new life] in Christ!
AMP.2: I fed you with milk, not solid food; for you were not yet able to receive it. Even now you are still not ready.
AMP.3: You are still worldly [controlled by ordinary impulses, the sinful capacity]. For as long as there is jealousy and strife and discord among you, are you not unspiritual, and are you not walking like ordinary men [unchanged by faith]?
AMP.4: For when one of you says, "I am [a disciple] of Paul," and another, "I am [a disciple] of Apollos," are you not [proving yourselves unchanged, just] ordinary people?
AMP.5: What then is Apollos? And what is Paul? Just servants through whom you believed [in Christ], even as the Lord appointed to each his task.
AMP.6: I planted, Apollos watered, but God [all the while] was causing the growth.
AMP.7: So neither is the one who plants nor the one who waters anything, but [only] God who causes the growth.
AMP.8: He who plants and he who waters are one [in importance and esteem, working toward the same purpose]; but each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.
AMP.9: For we are God's fellow workers [His servants working together]; you are God's cultivated field [His garden, His vineyard], God's building.
AMP.10: According to the [remarkable] grace of God which was given to me [to prepare me for my task], like a skillful master builder I laid a foundation, and now another is building on it. But each one must be careful how he builds on it,
AMP.11: for no one can lay a foundation other than the one which is [already] laid, which is Jesus Christ.
AMP.12: But if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, straw,
AMP.13: each one's work will be clearly shown [for what it is]; for the day [of judgment] will disclose it, because it is to be revealed with fire, and the fire will test the quality and character and worth of each person's work.
AMP.14: If any person's work which he has built [on this foundation, that is, any outcome of his effort] remains [and survives this test], he will receive a reward.
AMP.15: But if any person's work is burned up [by the test], he will suffer the loss [of his reward]; yet he himself will be saved, but only as [one who has barely escaped] through fire.
AMP.16: Do you not know and understand that you [the church] are the temple of God, and that the Spirit of God dwells [permanently] in you [collectively and individually]?
AMP.17: If anyone destroys the temple of God [corrupting it with false doctrine], God will destroy the destroyer; for the temple of God is holy (sacred), and that is what you are.
AMP.18: Let no one deceive himself. If anyone among you thinks that he is wise in this age, let him become a fool [discarding his worldly pretensions and acknowledging his lack of wisdom], so that he may become [truly] wise.
AMP.19: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness (absurdity, stupidity) before God; for it is written [in Scripture], "[He is] The one who catches the wise and clever in their craftiness;"
AMP.20: and again, "The Lord knows the thoughts of the [humanly] wise, that they are useless."
AMP.21: So let no one boast in men [about their wisdom, or of having this or that one as a leader]. For all things are yours,
AMP.22: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas (Peter) or the world or life or death or things present or things to come; all things are yours,
AMP.23: and you belong to Christ; and Christ belongs to God.

MSG.1: But for right now, friends, I'm completely frustrated by your unspiritual dealings with each other and with God. You're acting like infants in relation to Christ,
MSG.2: capable of nothing much more than nursing at the breast. Well, then, I'll nurse you since you don't seem capable of anything more.
MSG.3: As long as you grab for what makes you feel good or makes you look important, are you really much different than a babe at the breast, content only when everything's going your way?
MSG.4: When one of you says, "I'm on Paul's side," and another says, "I'm for Apollos," aren't you being totally infantile?
MSG.5: Who do you think Paul is, anyway? Or Apollos, for that matter? Servants, both of us—servants who waited on you as you gradually learned to entrust your lives to our mutual Master. We each carried out our servant assignment.
MSG.6: I planted the seed, Apollos watered the plants, but God made you grow.
MSG.7: It's not the one who plants or the one who waters who is at the center of this process but God, who makes things grow.
MSG.8: Planting and watering are menial servant jobs at minimum wages.
MSG.9: What makes them worth doing is the God we are serving. You happen to be God's field in which we are working. Or, to put it another way, you are God's house.
MSG.10: Using the gift God gave me as a good architect, I designed blueprints; Apollos is putting up the walls. Let each carpenter who comes on the job take care to build on the foundation!
MSG.11: Remember, there is only one foundation, the one already laid: Jesus Christ.
MSG.12: Take particular care in picking out your building materials.
MSG.13: Eventually there is going to be an inspection. If you use cheap or inferior materials, you'll be found out. The inspection will be thorough and rigorous. You won't get by with a thing.
MSG.14: If your work passes inspection, fine;
MSG.15: if it doesn't, your part of the building will be torn out and started over. But you won't be torn out; you'll survive—but just barely.
MSG.16: You realize, don't you, that you are the temple of God, and God himself is present in you?
MSG.17: No one will get by with vandalizing God's temple, you can be sure of that. God's temple is sacred—and you, remember, are the temple.
MSG.18: Don't fool yourself. Don't think that you can be wise merely by being up-to-date with the times.
MSG.19: Be God's fool—that's the path to true wisdom. What the world calls smart, God calls stupid. It's written in Scripture, He exposes the chicanery of the chic.
MSG.20: The Master sees through the smoke screens of the know-it-alls.
MSG.21: I don't want to hear any of you bragging about yourself or anyone else. Everything is already yours as a gift—
MSG.22: Paul, Apollos, Peter, the world, life, death, the present, the future—all of it is yours,
MSG.23: and you are privileged to be in union with Christ, who is in union with God.

NIV.1:
On Divisions in the Church
Brothers, I could not address you as spiritual but as worldly—mere infants in Christ.
NIV.2: I gave you milk, not solid food, for you were not yet ready for it. Indeed, you are still not ready.
NIV.3: You are still worldly. For since there is jealousy and quarreling among you, are you not worldly? Are you not acting like mere men?
NIV.4: For when one says, “I follow Paul,” and another, “I follow Apollos,” are you not mere men?
NIV.5: What, after all, is Apollos? And what is Paul? Only servants, through whom you came to believe—as the Lord has assigned to each his task.
NIV.6: I planted the seed, Apollos watered it, but God made it grow.
NIV.7: So neither he who plants nor he who waters is anything, but only God, who makes things grow.
NIV.8: The man who plants and the man who waters have one purpose, and each will be rewarded according to his own labor.
NIV.9: For we are God’s fellow workers; you are God’s field, God’s building.
NIV.10: By the grace God has given me, I laid a foundation as an expert builder, and someone else is building on it. But each one should be careful how he builds.
NIV.11: For no one can lay any foundation other than the one already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
NIV.12: If any man builds on this foundation using gold, silver, costly stones, wood, hay or straw,
NIV.13: his work will be shown for what it is, because the Day will bring it to light. It will be revealed with fire, and the fire will test the quality of each man’s work.
NIV.14: If what he has built survives, he will receive his reward.
NIV.15: If it is burned up, he will suffer loss; he himself will be saved, but only as one escaping through the flames.
NIV.16: Don’t you know that you yourselves are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit lives in you?
NIV.17: If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy him; for God’s temple is sacred, and you are that temple.
NIV.18: Do not deceive yourselves. If any one of you thinks he is wise by the standards of this age, he should become a “fool” so that he may become wise.
NIV.19: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness in God’s sight. As it is written: “He catches the wise in their craftiness”;
NIV.20: and again, “The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are futile.”
NIV.21: So then, no more boasting about men! All things are yours,
NIV.22: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas or the world or life or death or the present or the future—all are yours,
NIV.23: and you are of Christ, and Christ is of God.

NRSVCE.1: And so, brothers and sisters, I could not speak to you as spiritual people, but rather as people of the flesh, as infants in Christ.
NRSVCE.2: I fed you with milk, not solid food, for you were not ready for solid food. Even now you are still not ready,
NRSVCE.3: for you are still of the flesh. For as long as there is jealousy and quarreling among you, are you not of the flesh, and behaving according to human inclinations?
NRSVCE.4: For when one says, “I belong to Paul,” and another, “I belong to Apollos,” are you not merely human?
NRSVCE.5: What then is Apollos? What is Paul? Servants through whom you came to believe, as the Lord assigned to each.
NRSVCE.6: I planted, Apollos watered, but God gave the growth.
NRSVCE.7: So neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but only God who gives the growth.
NRSVCE.8: The one who plants and the one who waters have a common purpose, and each will receive wages according to the labor of each.
NRSVCE.9: For we are God’s servants, working together; you are God’s field, God’s building.
NRSVCE.10: According to the grace of God given to me, like a skilled master builder I laid a foundation, and someone else is building on it. Each builder must choose with care how to build on it.
NRSVCE.11: For no one can lay any foundation other than the one that has been laid; that foundation is Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.12: Now if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, straw —
NRSVCE.13: the work of each builder will become visible, for the Day will disclose it, because it will be revealed with fire, and the fire will test what sort of work each has done.
NRSVCE.14: If what has been built on the foundation survives, the builder will receive a reward.
NRSVCE.15: If the work is burned up, the builder will suffer loss; the builder will be saved, but only as through fire.
NRSVCE.16: Do you not know that you are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit dwells in you?
NRSVCE.17: If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy that person. For God’s temple is holy, and you are that temple.
NRSVCE.18: Do not deceive yourselves. If you think that you are wise in this age, you should become fools so that you may become wise.
NRSVCE.19: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, “He catches the wise in their craftiness,”
NRSVCE.20:
and again, “The Lord knows the thoughts of the wise,
that they are futile.”
NRSVCE.21: So let no one boast about human leaders. For all things are yours,
NRSVCE.22: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas or the world or life or death or the present or the future — all belong to you,
NRSVCE.23: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.

NET.1: So, brothers and sisters, I could not speak to you as spiritual people, but instead as people of the flesh, as infants in Christ.
NET.2: I fed you milk, not solid food, for you were not yet ready. In fact, you are still not ready,
NET.3: for you are still influenced by the flesh. For since there is still jealousy and dissension among you, are you not influenced by the flesh and behaving like unregenerate people?
NET.4: For whenever someone says, “I am with Paul,” or “I am with Apollos,” are you not merely human?
NET.5: What is Apollos, really? Or what is Paul? Servants through whom you came to believe, and each of us in the ministry the Lord gave us.
NET.6: I planted, Apollos watered, but God caused it to grow.
NET.7: So neither the one who plants counts for anything, nor the one who waters, but God who causes the growth.
NET.8: The one who plants and the one who waters work as one, but each will receive his reward according to his work.
NET.9: We are coworkers belonging to God. You are God’s field, God’s building.
NET.10: According to the grace of God given to me, like a skilled master-builder I laid a foundation, but someone else builds on it. And each one must be careful how he builds.
NET.11: For no one can lay any foundation other than what is being laid, which is Jesus Christ.
NET.12: If anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, or straw,
NET.13: each builder’s work will be plainly seen, for the Day will make it clear, because it will be revealed by fire. And the fire will test what kind of work each has done.
NET.14: If what someone has built survives, he will receive a reward.
NET.15: If someone’s work is burned up, he will suffer loss. He himself will be saved, but only as through fire.
NET.16: Do you not know that you are God’s temple and that God’s Spirit lives in you?
NET.17: If someone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy him. For God’s temple is holy, which is what you are.
NET.18: Guard against self-deception, each of you. If someone among you thinks he is wise in this age, let him become foolish so that he can become wise.
NET.19: For the wisdom of this age is foolishness with God. As it is written, “He catches the wise in their craftiness.”
NET.20: And again, “The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are futile.”
NET.21: So then, no more boasting about mere mortals! For everything belongs to you,
NET.22: whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas or the world or life or death or the present or the future. Everything belongs to you,
NET.23: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
